title: Epic: BD Podcast Distribution Pipeline (Phase 1)
description:
Business Decisions Phase 1: automated podcast-to-social-media pipeline.
SKILL-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE: NO AI API keys. All AI thinking runs in user's Claude Code subscription via skills. Server endpoints are RECEIVERS (validate + save), never CALLERS of AI.
7 steps: Transcribe (Whisper) -> Select Clips (SKILL) -> Cut Clips (ffmpeg) -> Generate Metadata (SKILL) -> Distribute (Upload-Post) -> Collect Analytics -> Generate Insights (SKILL).
Steps 2, 4, 7 are skill-driven: Claude Code reads data, thinks, POSTs results to server endpoints.
Architecture: Step-based workflows, PostgreSQL source of truth, R2 object storage, Upload-Post for posting.
Each step = independent feature folder + API endpoint + DB state transition.
13 accounts across TikTok, Instagram, Facebook, X, YouTube.
Target: <2 hours from recording to all posts distributed.
close_reason: Epic complete. All 22 beads closed. SKILL-DRIVEN ARCHITECTURE: zero AI API keys. 7 pipeline steps (4 providers, 7 feature endpoints, 5 Claude Code skills, 1 seed script). 138+ tests pass, tsc clean. Ready for Phase 1 dogfooding — run /process-episode to process first podcast.

title: Enable Better Auth email verification + password recovery
description:
Configure Better Auth's built-in email verification and password recovery.

BACKGROUND: Better Auth handles all token management, storage, expiry, and hashing. We only provide email-sending callbacks and email templates. No custom token management.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- MODIFY platform/auth/config.ts:

1. Email verification:
   emailAndPassword: {
     enabled: true,
     requireEmailVerification: true,
   },
   emailVerification: {
     sendOnSignUp: true,
     autoSignInAfterVerification: true,
     expiresIn: 86400, // 24 hours
     sendVerificationEmail: async ({ user, url }) => {
       await sendEmail(user.email, verificationEmail({ name: user.name, url }))
     },
   },

2. Password recovery:
   Add to emailAndPassword config:
     sendResetPassword: async ({ user, url }) => {
       await sendEmail(user.email, passwordResetEmail({ name: user.name, url }))
     },
     resetPasswordTokenExpiresIn: 1800, // 30 minutes
     revokeSessionsOnPasswordReset: true,
     onPasswordReset: async ({ user }) => {
       await sendEmail(user.email, passwordChangedEmail({ name: user.name, date: new Date().toISOString(), forgotPasswordUrl: env.PUBLIC_APP_URL + '/forgot-password' }))
     },

IMPORTS:
- import { sendEmail } from '@/providers/email'
- import { verificationEmail, passwordResetEmail, passwordChangedEmail } from '@/features/(shared)/email/auth-emails'
- import { env } from '@/platform/env'

title: Rewrite subscription linking to use session_id
description:
Rewrite the success page subscription linking to use Stripe session_id instead of email matching.

BACKGROUND: Current link-subscription.ts links the most recent unlinked subscription (naive). The eng review decided: use session_id from URL for deterministic linking. Success page is the PRIMARY path for subscription creation (webhook is idempotent backup).

WHAT TO BUILD:
- MODIFY features/(life)/onboarding/link-subscription.ts (or create new endpoint):

New flow (POST /api/checkout/complete):
1. Receive { sessionId, name, password } from success page form
2. Retrieve Stripe Checkout session: payments.checkout.sessions.retrieve(sessionId)
3. Get stripeCustomerId + stripeSubscriptionId from session
4. Get customer email from session (for pre-fill)
5. Create subscription in DB:
   db.insert(subscriptions).values({ stripeCustomerId, stripeSubscriptionId, status: 'active', currentPeriodEnd }).onConflictDoNothing()
   (Webhook may have already created it -- idempotent)
6. Create user account via Better Auth (email from Stripe session, name + password from form)
7. Link: db.update(subscriptions).set({ userId: user.id }).where(eq(stripeSubscriptionId))
8. Update user role: db.update(users).set({ role: 'pro' })
9. Send paymentConfirmationEmail with first lesson link
10. Return success -> redirect to course dashboard

EDGE CASES:
- Invalid/expired session_id -> clear error (throwError VALIDATION_ERROR)
- session_id already used (user created, subscription linked) -> redirect to login

title: Configure Stripe Dashboard: Smart Retries + dunning + invoice timing
description:
Configure Stripe Dashboard settings for dunning and renewal reminders. These are manual steps, no code.

BACKGROUND: Three Stripe Dashboard settings must be configured BEFORE the webhook code that depends on them:

1. SMART RETRIES:
   - Stripe Dashboard -> Billing -> Revenue Recovery
   - Enable Smart Retries (Stripe ML picks optimal retry times)
   - This alone recovers significant portion of failed payments silently

2. FAILED PAYMENT -> CANCEL AFTER 14 DAYS:
   - Stripe Dashboard -> Billing -> Subscription and emails -> Manage failed payments
   - Set: retry for 14 days, then cancel subscription
   - This is our grace period -- Stripe controls the timing, zero code needed

3. INVOICE.UPCOMING TIMING:
   - Stripe Dashboard -> Billing -> Subscription and emails -> Days before renewal
   - Set to 7 days (default is 3 days)
   - This controls when the invoice.upcoming webhook fires for our renewal reminder email

4. CUSTOMER PORTAL:
   - Stripe Dashboard -> Settings -> Customer Portal
   - Enable: Update payment method, View invoice history, Cancel subscription
   - Brand colors: primary #C4956A, background #FAF8F5
   - Add logo
   - Cancellation: ask reason (optional), cancel at period end

ACCEPTANCE:
- Smart Retries enabled
- Failed payments cancel after 14 days
- invoice.upcoming fires 7 days before charge
- Customer Portal configured with brand colors
close_reason: Stripe Dashboard config is a manual task. Settings documented in document.md Section 7-8. Ready for manual configuration.

BACKGROUND: Currently requirePermission only checks user.role (free/pro/admin). If role is 'pro' but subscription is 'cancelled', user still has full access. Dunning and revocation are meaningless without this enforcement layer.

WHY NOT JUST CHANGE THE ROLE? Role changes are one-way and hard to undo. If we set role to 'free' on cancellation, we need custom logic to set it back to 'pro' when they resubscribe. The middleware approach checks subscription status in real-time.

WHAT TO BUILD:

- Export: requireActiveSubscription middleware for Hono
- Flow:
  1. Get userId from context (after requireAuth)
  2. Query subscription by userId (most recent)
  3. Map status to access:
     - active -> allow (next())
     - past_due -> allow (grace period, 14 days while Stripe retries)
     - trialing -> allow
     - cancelled -> deny (throwError 'SUBSCRIPTION_REQUIRED')
     - no subscription found -> deny
  4. Set subscription data in context for downstream use

- MODIFY course routes to stack: requireAuth -> requireActiveSubscription -> handler

IMPORTS:

- import { subscriptions } from '@/platform/db/schema'

title: Harden webhook handler: idempotency + new events + logging
description:
Expand the Stripe webhook handler with idempotency, 3 new event handlers, email sending, and logging.

BACKGROUND: Current handler has 3 events, no idempotency, no logging, and conflates subscription statuses. This is the core payment infrastructure.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. IDEMPOTENCY (INSERT-first pattern):
   - At top of handler, after signature verification:
   - INSERT into webhookEvents with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
   - If rowCount === 0, event already processed -> return success immediately
   - One DB call for new events, one for duplicates

2. NEW EVENT HANDLERS:
   a. invoice.payment_succeeded:
      - Check billing_reason === 'subscription_cycle' (renewal, not first payment)
      - If renewal + getUserForSubscription returns user -> send renewalReceiptEmail
      - Update subscription currentPeriodEnd
   b. invoice.payment_failed:
      - Set subscription status to 'past_due'
      - If user exists -> send paymentFailedEmail with Customer Portal link
      - Log failure reason from invoice
   c. invoice.upcoming:
      - If user exists -> send renewalReminderEmail
      - Stripe configured to fire 7 days before charge (Dashboard setting)

3. FIX customer.subscription.updated:
   - Preserve Stripe's actual status (not conflate to past_due)
   - If cancel_at_period_end set -> send subscriptionCancelledEmail
   - Otherwise: map active->active, past_due->past_due

4. FIX customer.subscription.deleted:
   - Distinguish user-initiated vs involuntary:
     - If last status was past_due -> involuntary -> send accessRevokedEmail
     - Otherwise -> user-initiated -> send subscriptionCancelledEmail (if not already sent)

5. LOGGING: console.info('[webhook] event.type event.id') for every event.

6. CRITICAL: Wrap ALL sendEmail calls in try/catch. If Resend is down, log the error but still return success(c, { received: true }). Otherwise Stripe retries indefinitely.

FILES:
- MODIFY features/(shared)/subscription/handle-webhook.ts

ACCEPTANCE:
- Idempotency: same event ID processed twice = 1 DB write, 0 duplicate emails
- invoice.payment_succeeded with billing_reason=subscription_create -> no email (skip)
- invoice.payment_succeeded with billing_reason=subscription_cycle -> renewal receipt email
- invoice.payment_failed -> status=past_due + payment-failed email
- invoice.upcoming -> renewal reminder email
- customer.subscription.updated with cancel_at_period_end -> cancelled email
- customer.subscription.deleted (involuntary) -> access-revoked email
- customer.subscription.deleted (user-initiated) -> cancelled email
- sendEmail failure inside webhook does NOT cause webhook to fail
- All events logged
- All tests pass
close_reason: Hardened webhook handler: INSERT-first idempotency, 3 new event handlers (invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, invoice.upcoming), fixed status conflation, user-initiated vs involuntary cancellation distinction, safeSendEmail wrapper, logging. All tests pass.

title: Add A/B headline variant logic to landing route
description:
Add A/B headline variant logic using cookie persistence.

WHAT: Update landing page route for headline variant selection.

WHY: 4 headline variants for A/B testing per PRD.

NOTE: The PRD mentions localStorage but we use server-side cookie instead because:
- Landing page is SSR (no client JS) -- localStorage requires JS
- Cookie is sent on every request, server reads it directly
- httpOnly cookie is more secure than localStorage

IMPLEMENTATION in features/(life)/landing/routes.ts:
```tsx
import { getCookie, setCookie } from 'hono/cookie'

const VARIANTS = ['a', 'b', 'c', 'd'] as const
type Variant = (typeof VARIANTS)[number]

landingRoutes.get('/', (c) => {
  // Priority: URL param > cookie > random
  let variant = c.req.query('v') as Variant | undefined

  if (!variant || !VARIANTS.includes(variant)) {
    variant = getCookie(c, 'lp_variant') as Variant | undefined
  }

  if (!variant || !VARIANTS.includes(variant)) {
    variant = VARIANTS[Math.floor(Math.random() * VARIANTS.length)]
  }

  // Persist for 30 days
  setCookie(c, 'lp_variant', variant, {
    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30,
    path: '/',
    httpOnly: true,
    sameSite: 'Lax',
  })

  return c.html(renderPage(<LandingPage variant={variant} />, {
    title: 'The Right Decision -- Life Decisions Course',
    description: 'A methodology + AI that turns stuck goals into clear decisions. $197/year. 7-day guarantee.',
  }))
})
```

SEO TITLE/DESC (from PRD Section 5):
- title: "The Right Decision -- Life Decisions Course"
- description: "A methodology + AI that turns stuck goals into clear decisions. $197/year. 7-day guarantee."

${html}
` } ``` KEY DETAILS FROM PRD: - SEO: og:title, og:description, og:image, og:type tags - Accessibility: skip-navigation link for screen readers (PRD Section 5) - Fonts: font-display: swap (design.md Typography section) - Semantic HTML:

title: Build markdown content rendering system for blog and concepts
description:
Build core content rendering: reads markdown files with frontmatter from filesystem and renders as SSR HTML. Foundation for blog posts, concept pages, and legal pages.

BACKGROUND: Doc 11 Section 5 — files are the CMS. Markdown in content/blog/, content/concepts/, and content/legal/ become web pages. Follows architecture.md data storage rule: 'AI agents author it AND humans review it AND git versioning matters -> filesystem.'

REASONING: Using markdown files instead of a database means: (1) AI agents can create content via file writes, (2) git tracks all revisions, (3) PRs review content changes, (4) no CMS to maintain. The markdown provider is the single abstraction all content routes use.

WHAT TO BUILD:
- CREATE providers/markdown.ts:
  - parseFrontmatter(raw: string): { frontmatter: Record<string, unknown>, body: string } — splits YAML frontmatter from markdown body
  - renderMarkdown(body: string): string — converts markdown to HTML using 'marked' library. Generates heading IDs for anchor links (slugify headings). Adds target=_blank rel=noopener to external links.
  - listContentFiles(dir: string): Promise<ParsedContent[]> — lists all .md files, parses frontmatter, filters status=published, sorts by date descending. Returns array of { frontmatter, slug } (no body — for index pages).
  - getContentFile(dir: string, slug: string): Promise<ParsedContent | null> — reads specific file, parses frontmatter + renders body to HTML. Returns null if not found or status=draft.
  - calculateReadTime(body: string): number — words / 200, rounded up to nearest minute
  - Zod schemas exported for validation:
    - BlogPostFrontmatter: title(string), slug(string), description(string), author(enum henry|indy|henry-and-indy), date(string ISO), updated?(string ISO), cluster(enum feeling-stuck|anti-self-help|decision-making|life-transitions), tags?(string[]), keywords(string[]), status(enum draft|published)
    - ConceptFrontmatter: title(string), slug(string), description(string), keywords(string[]), relatedConcepts?(string[]), internalConcept?(string), faq(array of {question: string, answer: string}), status(enum draft|published)
    - LegalFrontmatter: title(string), slug(string), description(string), date(string ISO)

CONSIDERATIONS:
- Use 'marked' for markdown rendering (lightweight, fast, Bun-compatible). Install: bun add marked
- Content directories: content/blog/, content/concepts/, content/legal/
- File naming convention: slug.md (file name = slug, no transformation needed)
- Draft filtering: listContentFiles and getContentFile both exclude status=draft
- Error handling: getContentFile returns null for missing files (caller decides 404 behavior)
- Frontmatter parsing: use 'gray-matter' or manual YAML between --- delimiters. Install: bun add gray-matter
- Heading IDs: 'My Heading' -> id='my-heading' for anchor links
- Read time: 200 words per minute is standard

FILES:
- CREATE providers/markdown.ts
- CREATE providers/markdown.test.ts
- CREATE content/blog/.gitkeep
- CREATE content/concepts/.gitkeep
- CREATE content/legal/.gitkeep

TEST OBLIGATIONS:
- parseFrontmatter: valid file with YAML, file without frontmatter (throws), malformed YAML
- renderMarkdown: headings get IDs, bold/italic/links/lists/code, external links get target=_blank
- listContentFiles: returns only published, sorted by date desc, excludes drafts
- getContentFile: returns parsed content for valid slug, returns null for missing, returns null for draft
- calculateReadTime: 200 words = 1 min, 500 words = 3 min
- Zod schemas: valid data passes, missing required fields fail, enum validation works

ACCEPTANCE:
- parseFrontmatter extracts YAML frontmatter + body correctly
- renderMarkdown converts to clean HTML with heading IDs and safe external links
- listContentFiles returns sorted published files (filters drafts)
- getContentFile returns parsed frontmatter + rendered HTML for slug, null for missing/draft
- calculateReadTime returns correct minute estimates
- All three Zod schemas validate correctly
- All tests pass (10+ test cases)

title: Epic: 10-Star Editorial Course Experience (Doc 12)
description:
Transform the course platform from 'functional MVP with plain text rendering' into a 10-star editorial reading experience. The course is the unboxing for the Claude skills (the real product). A cheap unboxing undercuts trust before the real product gets a chance.

CORE THESIS: Premium through subtraction, not addition. The ICP has taken every Kajabi course. Another dashboard triggers 'here we go again.' The course should feel like reading a beautifully designed book, not taking an online course.

EUREKA: No course platform in the landscape does what we're building. This is the first course where the reading experience itself practices the skill being taught (deciding) through in-class micro-decisions.

6 PHASES:
Phase 0: Validate + Spike (marked rendering, phone test)
Phase 1: Core Craft (MDX rendering, design tokens, reading room, chapter dashboard, mobile bottom nav)
Phase 2: Multi-Course Architecture (content provider refactor, courses.json, course listing)
Phase 3: Micro-Decisions (userDecisions table, component, API, 5-min edit window)
Phase 4: Journey + Analytics (Your Journey page, reading analytics, timeline)
Phase 5: Editorial + Art (pull quotes, callouts, drop caps, AI illustrations, transitions)
Phase 6: Share + Polish (share moments via satori, reading time, session memory)

SOURCE: decisions/12-courses-ux-upgrade/document.md
REVIEWS: office-hours (APPROVED), CEO (6/6 expansions), eng (0 critical gaps), design (7→9/10)
TOTAL: 14-22 wall-clock days. Each phase ships independently.

title: Build micro-decisions service (save, get, edit window)
description:
Build the micro-decisions service layer with save/get/edit logic and the 5-minute edit window.

BACKGROUND: The micro-decision is where the user practices deciding within the course. Each class has at most one decision prompt. After the user submits, they get a 5-minute edit window, then the decision is locked forever. This reinforces the "decide and move" philosophy.

WHAT TO BUILD:
1. Service functions in features/(life)/course/decisions.ts:
   - saveDecision(userId, classId, courseSlug, decisionType, prompt, response) → decision
     - Check if decision exists for this user+class
     - If exists and < 5 min old: update (edit window)
     - If exists and >= 5 min old: throw DECISION_LOCKED
     - If not exists: insert
   - getDecision(userId, classId) → decision | null
   - getUserDecisions(userId, courseSlug?) → decision[]
   - isDecisionEditable(decision) → boolean (createdAt + 5 min > now)

2. API routes in features/(life)/course/decision-routes.ts:
   - POST /api/decisions/save — save or update a decision
     - Body: { classId, courseSlug, decisionType, prompt, response }
     - Requires auth (requireAuth middleware)
     - Returns: { decision, editable: boolean }
   - GET /api/decisions/:classId — get user's decision for a class
     - Requires auth
     - Returns: { decision, editable: boolean } or 404
   - GET /api/decisions — get all user's decisions (for Journey page)
     - Requires auth
     - Query: ?courseSlug=life-decisions (optional filter)
     - Returns: { decisions: Decision[] }

3. Wire routes in platform/server/routes.ts

FILES:
- CREATE features/(life)/course/decisions.ts — service functions
- CREATE features/(life)/course/decisions.test.ts — TDD tests
- CREATE features/(life)/course/decision-routes.ts — API routes
- MODIFY platform/server/routes.ts — mount decision routes

BUILD ORDER: Steps 5-7 (tests → code → refactor)

5-MINUTE EDIT WINDOW LOGIC:
```ts
const EDIT_WINDOW_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000 // 5 minutes
function isEditable(decision: UserDecision): boolean {
  return Date.now() - decision.createdAt.getTime() < EDIT_WINDOW_MS
}
```

SECURITY: Sanitize response text (strip HTML tags) — user input will be rendered on Journey page and share cards.

title: Build reading analytics service (client + server)
description:
Build the reading analytics system: client-side tracking + server-side storage.

BACKGROUND: Reading analytics is tiny effort, huge product intelligence. Track time spent per class, scroll depth, and completion. Fire-and-forget: analytics failure must never impact reading experience.

WHAT TO BUILD:

CLIENT SIDE:
1. Vanilla JS module (<1KB) loaded in ClassView:
   - IntersectionObserver for scroll depth tracking (0-100%)
   - Timer for time spent (pause on tab hidden, resume on visible)
   - Send analytics on: page unload (sendBeacon), every 30s heartbeat, scroll milestones (25/50/75/100%)
   - Store in-progress data in sessionStorage (survives page refreshes within session)

SERVER SIDE:
2. Service functions in features/(life)/course/reading-analytics.ts:
   - logReading(userId, classId, courseSlug, timeSpentSec, scrollDepth) → void
     - UPSERT: if exists, update timeSpentSec (add) and scrollDepth (max)
     - Set completedAt when scrollDepth >= 90%
   - getReadingStats(userId, courseSlug?) → { totalTimeMin, classesRead, avgTimePerClass }

3. API route:
   - POST /api/analytics/reading — log reading data (fire-and-forget)
     - Body: { classId, courseSlug, timeSpentSec, scrollDepth }
     - Requires auth
     - Returns 200 immediately (swallow errors, log them)
   - Error handling: swallow + log (fire-and-forget per eng review decision)

title: Create userDecisions database table and migration
description:
Create the userDecisions table for storing in-class micro-decision responses.

BACKGROUND: The micro-decision moment is THE 10-star differentiator. Users make one decision per class — this is the first course where the reading experience practices the skill being taught. Decisions are stored permanently and shown on the Journey page.

WHAT TO BUILD:
Add to platform/db/schema.ts:
```ts
export const userDecisions = pgTable('user_decisions', {
  id: uuid('id').defaultRandom().primaryKey(),
  userId: uuid('user_id').notNull().references(() => users.id, { onDelete: 'cascade' }),
  classId: text('class_id').notNull(),
  courseSlug: text('course_slug').notNull(),
  decisionType: text('decision_type', { enum: ['text', 'choice'] }).notNull(),
  prompt: text('prompt').notNull(),
  response: text('response').notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
  updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').notNull().defaultNow(),
}, (table) => [
  uniqueIndex('user_decisions_user_class_idx').on(table.userId, table.classId),
])
```

KEY DECISION: 5-minute edit window uses createdAt comparison. After 5 min from createdAt, the decision is locked. This handles typos without contradicting "decide and move."

FILES:
- MODIFY platform/db/schema.ts — add userDecisions table
- Run: bun run db:generate && bun run db:migrate

BUILD ORDER: Step 2 (schema)

ACCEPTANCE:
- userDecisions table defined in schema.ts
- UNIQUE constraint on (userId, classId) — one decision per class
- Foreign key to users with CASCADE delete
- Migration generates and runs cleanly
- Types infer correctly from Drizzle schema
close_reason: Added userDecisions table to schema.ts with UNIQUE(userId, classId), CASCADE delete, text/choice enum.
